user_query:
We’re planning Q3 merchandising tests and need you to model AOV and gross margin impacts for three offer ideas before we greenlight anything. Please read our baseline and scenario assumptions from:
- input/baseline.json
- input/scenarios.json

Assumptions to use in all calculations:
- Define AOV as gross order value before refunds and taxes.
- Compute gross margin rate as (expected revenue − expected COGS) / expected revenue, where expected COGS uses unit_cost × expected units.
- Treat attach rates as probabilities applied to orders that convert on the main product; expected revenue/COGS should reflect those probabilities.
- Only apply the scenario-specific changes described in input/scenarios.json; otherwise keep baseline values.
- Assume no shipping, tax, or variable costs beyond the provided unit costs.
- Use sessions × conversion rate to compute expected order count for each scenario.

Required deliverables under output/:
1) Write a structured results file to output/results.json with the following schema and content:
{
  "assumptions": { "aov_definition": "gross_pre_refund", "currency": "USD" },
  "baseline": {
    "orders": <number>,
    "aov": <number>,
    "revenue": <number>,
    "gross_margin_rate": <number>,
    "gross_profit": <number>
  },
  "scenarios": [
    {
      "name": "bundle_offer",
      "orders": <number>,
      "aov": <number>,
      "revenue": <number>,
      "gross_margin_rate": <number>,
      "gross_profit": <number>,
      "delta_vs_baseline": { "aov_abs": <number>, "gross_profit_abs": <number> }
    },
    {
      "name": "upsell_offer",
      "orders": <number>,
      "aov": <number>,
      "revenue": <number>,
      "gross_margin_rate": <number>,
      "gross_profit": <number>,
      "delta_vs_baseline": { "aov_abs": <number>, "gross_profit_abs": <number> }
    },
    {
      "name": "price_increase_5",
      "orders": <number>,
      "aov": <number>,
      "revenue": <number>,
      "gross_margin_rate": <number>,
      "gross_profit": <number>,
      "delta_vs_baseline": { "aov_abs": <number>, "gross_profit_abs": <number> }
    }
  ],
  "break_even": {
    "price_increase_5": {
      "max_conversion_drop_pct_to_match_baseline_gp": <number>
    }
  }
}
- For bundle_offer, model a bundle for the specified items at the specified bundle_price applied to the specified bundle_take_rate share of orders; conversion rate otherwise unchanged. For the bundle segment, the bundled accessory is guaranteed attached at the bundle price. Other attach rates remain as specified. For non-bundle orders, use baseline attach rates.
- For upsell_offer, raise the specified item’s attach rate to the new_attach_rate; conversion rate otherwise unchanged.
- For price_increase_5, increase only the specified item’s price by the given percentage and multiply the conversion rate by the provided conversion_multiplier; attach rates unchanged.
- Also compute the break-even for price_increase_5: the maximum conversion drop percentage at which scenario gross profit equals baseline gross profit. Include this in results.json at break_even.price_increase_5.max_conversion_drop_pct_to_match_baseline_gp (as a percent number, e.g., 7.14 for 7.14%).

2) Write a clear 5-part decision report to output/report.md using exactly these section headers:
- Baseline view
- Scenario modeling results
- Margin and break-even implications
- Key risks and weak points
- Recommendation

The report should concisely explain the baseline economics, compare all three scenarios (bundle_offer, upsell_offer, and price_increase_5), discuss margin tradeoffs and break-even logic (including the conversion break-even for the price test), call out the biggest risks/assumptions, and give a go/watch/no-go recommendation with guardrails for a limited test.

Please make sure your numbers in output/results.json are consistent with the inputs in input/baseline.json and input/scenarios.json, and reference all three scenario names explicitly in output/report.md.
